title: Microlicia ordinata
taxonomy: Microlicia ordinata belongs to the family Melastomataceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the melastome or Memecyclaceae. Microlicia ordinata, along with other members of Melastomataceae, is classified as part of the order Myrtales in the angiospermic plant taxonomy. The plant's classification places it within the larger group of eudicots. These classifications are based on the plant's morphological and molecular characteristics.
uses: Microlicia ordinata is known to have various medicinal uses, with its extracts and compounds having potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's leaves and stems have been used in traditional medicine in Brazil, where it is native, to treat a range of health issues. Research has shown that the plant contains flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that may contribute to its medicinal properties. Further studies are needed to fully understand the plant's potential benefits and applications. The plant's medicinal uses are still being studied and researched, but initial findings are promising.
